verdicts.note: The diff now passes a narrow run_bookkeeping_excludes list into dirty checks, backups, rollback cleanliness checks, and commits, excluding only the live run_dir and active-run pointer. The broad reset_to_base git clean exclusion is left only in the intentionally ratified cleanup path.

Rollback now reloads the pipeline order and resets every step record after the target commit step to pending, not just dropped commit-step records. That prevents resume from skipping downstream work removed by git reset.
Rollback now requires HEAD to exactly equal the manifest's last recorded commit before rewinding. A branch ahead of the manifest is rejected instead of silently discarded.
Step-level foreach now skips done/skipped iteration records on resume. Parked gate lookup scans iteration records, so a human_gate parked inside foreach can be approved or rejected by step id.
Validation now treats on_fail.route_to as stage-local, matching the runtime routing model. Cross-stage targets are rejected at load instead of crashing when routed.

max_turns is now rejected at pipeline load for steps and referenced profiles, matching the ratified unsupported-CLI decision. Shell timeout_s now halts with HALTED, and agent_task picks up profile step_timeout_s as a fallback.
The entry contract now compares the marker-stripped, normalized PRD body against the scaffold. Deleting only the marker line is refused.

The drafter now sees untracked-file status, optional plan_section content, and the final draft result's usage/session are written into the commit step record. However the redraft loop overwrites usage on each attempt, so usage from rejected draft calls is still omitted from manifest totals.
ManagedJudge now snapshots and restores the managed judge environment variables, including GAUNTLET_STEP_ID. The added tests cover clearing unset vars and restoring prior values.

summary: The diff resolves 8 of 9 findings. F-008 remains partially resolved because commit-message redraft accounting still drops usage from earlier rejected draft attempts.
